question: Devils vs. Bruins: O/U 4.5
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 14 at 7:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Devils and Bruins combine to score 5 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 5, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Boston Bruins enter as clear home favorites against the New Jersey Devils, with trader consensus reflecting their stronger recent form (5-2-1 in last eight) and elite goaltending from Jeremy Swayman, who's posted a .925 save percentage lately. Devils' momentum has stalled amid Jack Hughes' upper-body injury sidelining him for a third straight game, weakening their top-line scoring punch against Boston's stout penalty kill. Bruins boast a perfect 3-0 home record this season, while New Jersey struggles on the road (1-3). Watch for Devils' power play exploitation if Boston's discipline slips, but rest advantage tilts odds toward a low-scoring affair under 6.0 total goals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Minnesota Wild's superior standing in the Central Division playoff race, bolstered by a 4-1-1 recent run and elite goaltending from Filip Gustavsson, anchors trader consensus favoring them heavily against the rebuilding Anaheim Ducks, who sit last in the Pacific with a 3-6-1 skid. Official injury reports confirm Ducks forward Trevor Zegras remains sidelined with an ankle issue, thinning their top-six scoring, while Wild captain Jared Spurgeon returns from suspension, strengthening their blue line. Home-ice edge at Xcel Energy Center and Wild's 6-2-2 home record amplify the edge, though Ducks' youth could spark an upset amid Wild's occasional lapses against weaker foes. Schedule density favors rested Wild post-bye.

eventMetadata.context_description: Winnipeg Jets face Utah Hockey Club in a pivotal Central Division NHL clash, where trader consensus leans toward Utah amid the Jets' 2-5-0 start plagued by injuries to Nikolaj Ehlers (knee, out indefinitely) and Kyle Connor's recent lower-body issue limiting practice time. Utah (5-2-1) rides momentum from a four-game win streak at home, bolstered by Clayton Keller's hot hand (10 points) and Vitek Vanecek's .915 save percentage in net. Jets rely on Connor Hellebuyck's Vezina form but face back-to-back fatigue after a loss to Dallas; Utah's rest edge and 3-1 home record vs. Jets historically tilt probabilities, though upsets loom in high-scoring affairs averaging 7.2 goals.

eventMetadata.context_description: Colorado Avalanche hold a commanding edge as home favorites against the Calgary Flames, driven by their league-leading offense averaging over four goals per game and Nathan MacKinnon's eight points in four contests. Recent developments favor Colorado: a three-game win streak, including a 6-3 rout of Vegas, contrasts Calgary's 1-3-0 skid marked by defensive lapses allowing 4.5 goals per outing. No major injuries reported for either side per official updates, though Flames goaltender Dustin Wolf faces a tough test in net. Trader consensus reflects Avalanche's 65% implied win probability, tempered by Calgary's desperation for a road upset amid early Pacific Division woes.

question: Will 'BULLY' - Ye debut week album sales be less than 300k?
description:
Kanye West's new album 'BULLY' is scheduled to be released on March 27, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the debut week sales for Kanye West's album 'BULLY', according to Hits Daily Double.

If the album 'BULLY' has not been released by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.
 
If the album’s debut week sales fall exactly between two brackets, this market will resolve to the higher bracket.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the "HITS TOP 50" list found at https://hitsdailydouble.com/sales_plus_streaming, specifically, the figure in the column titled "Activity", once the results are finalized for the album’s debut week. If this resolution source becomes permanently unavailable, another credible resolution source may be chosen.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors Kanye West's 'Bully' under 300k first-week US album sales at 82.5% implied probability, reflecting diminished commercial momentum from recent projects like Vultures 1 (148k debut) and Vultures 2 (43k), amid ongoing backlash from antisemitic remarks, brand severances, and distribution hurdles. Sparse promotion, no major radio airplay, and fragmented streaming rollout—split between Ye's YZY app and platforms—further dampen expectations, with historical precedents like Donda's 309k first week now outliers. Recent leaks and minimal pre-release buzz reinforce the sub-300k lead, while 300k-400k at 9% captures slim upside from core fan streaming; higher bins reflect outlier bundle potential absent confirmed retail tie-ins. Watch Billboard's Friday update for resolution.

question: T20I Series New Zealand vs South Africa, Women: New Zealand vs South Africa
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between New Zealand and South Africa scheduled for March 24 2026 in T20I Series New Zealand vs South Africa, Women.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: New Zealand Women enter the T20I series against South Africa Women as narrow favorites in trader consensus, buoyed by home advantage at Hagley Oval and a potent seam attack led by Lea Tahuhu and Jess Kerr, who thrive in seaming conditions. Recent squads confirm no major injuries, with Sophie Devine captaining a balanced NZ side fresh off domestic success, while South Africa relies on Laura Wolvaardt's batting and Nonkululeko Mlaba's spin post their T20 World Cup final run. Head-to-head records show NZ winning 7 of 12 T20Is, though SA's away form poses upset risk; schedule intensity peaks with back-to-back games in Christchurch, testing depth amid variable weather.
eventMetadata.league: T20I Series New Zealand vs South Africa, Women

question: T20I Series New Zealand vs South Africa, Women: New Zealand vs South Africa - Who wins the toss?
description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between New Zealand and South Africa scheduled for 2026-03-24 in T20I Series New Zealand vs South Africa, Women.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
If an official toss winner is recorded, the market will resolve based on that result even if the match is subsequently delayed, relocated, abandoned, or no ball is bowled.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market will remain open and resolve according to the toss result for the listed fixture when it is eventually played.
If the fixture is permanently canceled, forfeited before any toss is conducted, or otherwise completed without an official toss winner being recorded, the market will resolve 50-50.

question: T20I Series New Zealand vs South Africa, Women: New Zealand vs South Africa - Completed match?
description:
This market refers to whether the cricket match between New Zealand and South Africa scheduled for 2026-03-24 in T20I Series New Zealand vs South Africa, Women is completed under the competition's official playing conditions.
This market resolves according to the finalized match status as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
Any match where an official result is declared — including results decided by DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, a Super Over, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling — will resolve Yes. (Draws count as completed results when recognized by the competition.)
If the match is abandoned, ends in No Result, is permanently canceled, or otherwise concludes without an official result, the market will resolve No.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed or permanently canceled, after which it will resolve as described above.

question: T20I Series New Zealand vs South Africa, Women: New Zealand vs South Africa - Toss Match Double New Zealand Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between New Zealand and South Africa scheduled for 2026-03-24 in T20I Series New Zealand vs South Africa, Women.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to New Zealand will be considered correct if  New Zealand is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to South Africa will be considered correct if South Africa is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

eventMetadata.context_description: New Zealand Women host South Africa Women in the opening T20I of their series, with trader consensus reflecting a razor-thin 51.5% implied probability across White Ferns victory, Proteas win, and draw amid high uncertainty. Both sides enter evenly matched after strong recent form—New Zealand sweeping India 3-0 at home last month, South Africa clinching a T20I series against England—fueling the deadlock. Home advantage and spin-friendly pitches tilt slightly toward the hosts' Amelia Kerr and Lea Tahuhu, but South Africa's pace attack led by Marizanne Kapp and Tazmin Brits' batting exploits even the scales. Toss looms large for dew-affected chases, while light rain risks bolster draw pricing, underscoring the competitive balance in this high-stakes opener.

description:

description:

eventMetadata.context_description: New Zealand Women and South Africa Women enter their T20I showdown with closely matched six-hitting firepower, fueling trader consensus at 51.5% across NZL, ZAF, and draw outcomes for most sixes. Sophie Devine leads NZ's charge with 11 sixes across her last five T20Is, closely trailed by South Africa's Tazmin Brits (9) and Marizanne Kapp (8), per official ICC stats. This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to New Zealand will be considered correct if the highest individual score in the match is recorded exclusively by a player from New Zealand.
The outcome corresponding to South Africa will be considered correct if the highest individual score is recorded exclusively by a player from South Africa.
If the highest individual score is shared by at least one player from each team, the market will resolve to Draw.
The market will resolve according to which team's player records the highest individual run total regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions).
DLS/DRS adjustments and any other on-field rulings affecting batting statistics are treated as ordinary data.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official batting statistics being recorded, the market will resolve Draw.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the New Zealand Women vs South Africa Women T20I team top batter market shows even pricing at 51.5% across NZL, Draw, and ZAF outcomes, driven by comparable batting firepower and uncertain pitch behavior at Saxton Oval. New Zealand's Sophie Devine (recent 50+ scores) and Amelia Kerr anchor a middle order thriving in T20Is, while South Africa's Laura Wolvaardt (series-leading 200+ runs) and Tazmin Brits exploit powerplay aggression effectively. No major injuries reported, but variable bounce favors versatile stroke-makers from both sides, with head-to-head T20Is split 5-5 lately, keeping the race tight amid rest advantages for hosts.

question: Will the price of Solana be between $70 and $80 on March 25?
description:
This market will resolve according to the final "Close" price of the Binance 1 minute candle for SOL/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/SOL_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance SOL/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Saint Helena vs Seychelles
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Saint Helena and Seychelles scheduled for March 25 2026 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Seychelles holds a clear edge in trader consensus for the T20 World Cup Sub Regional Africa Qualifier B clash against Saint Helena, driven by their stronger recent form with two wins in three group games, including a 68-run victory over Cameroon, contrasting Saint Helena's three heavy defeats averaging under 70 runs batted. Both minnows lack deep T20 experience, but Seychelles boasts better bowling depth led by Deshius Ghanty (5 wickets in tournament) and a more reliable top order. The Entebbe pitch favors pace and low scores, amplifying Seychelles' 10-wicket haul advantage; no major injuries reported, though weather risks light rain. Historical head-to-head is nil, underscoring Seychelles' implied 75% win probability from crowd wisdom.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Saint Helena vs Seychelles - Most Sixes Saint Helena Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Saint Helena and Seychelles scheduled for 2026-03-25 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Saint Helena will be considered correct if Saint Helena is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Seychelles.The outcome corresponding to Seychelles will be considered correct if Seychelles is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Saint Helena.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tanzania enters as clear favorites in their T20 World Cup Sub Regional Africa Qualifier B clash with Eswatini, driven by superior recent form and head-to-head dominance. Tanzania sits atop Group B standings after emphatic wins over Seychelles (by 106 runs) and Ghana (by 8 wickets), boasting a potent batting lineup led by Abhik Patwa's explosive 81* and bowling spearheaded by Sanjay Kumar (4/10). question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Eswatini vs Tanzania - Toss Match Double Eswatini Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Eswatini and Tanzania scheduled for 2026-03-25 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Eswatini will be considered correct if  Eswatini is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Tanzania will be considered correct if Tanzania is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.

If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Eswatini vs Tanzania - Most Sixes Eswatini Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Eswatini and Tanzania scheduled for 2026-03-25 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Eswatini will be considered correct if Eswatini is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Tanzania.The outcome corresponding to Tanzania will be considered correct if Tanzania is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Eswatini.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the Eswatini vs Tanzania T20 World Cup Sub Regional Africa Qualifier B most sixes market hovers near even—SWZ at 50.5%, TZA at 50.0%, draw at 49.5%—driven by balanced batting firepower from both sides in recent outings. question: T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B: Malawi vs Ghana
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Malawi and Ghana scheduled for March 25 2026 in T20 World Cup, Sub Regional Africa, Qualifier B.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ghana holds a clear edge in trader consensus for the T20 World Cup Sub Regional Africa Qualifier B clash against Malawi, driven by their dominant 46-run victory over Malawi in their last meeting in December 2023, where Ghana posted 110/6 and bowled Malawi out for 64. Ghana's balanced attack, led by all-rounder Laweence Akyea and spinner Vincent Ateakayi, exploited Malawi's fragile top order, a persistent issue evident in recent losses to Nigeria and Rwanda. Malawi's pace bowler Usama Khan remains a threat, but their batting lacks depth amid inconsistent T20I form. Played on a spin-assisting pitch in Entebbe, Uganda, weather forecasts suggest minimal rain disruption, tilting implied probabilities toward Ghana amid both teams' push for Africa qualifier spots.

question: T20 Series New Zealand vs South Africa: New Zealand vs South Africa
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between New Zealand and South Africa scheduled for March 25 2026 in T20 Series New Zealand vs South Africa.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: New Zealand holds a strong edge in trader consensus for the opening T20I against South Africa, driven by their impeccable 8-0 home T20I record over the past two years and recent momentum from a 3-0 ODI sweep against Bangladesh. Wellington's seaming conditions favor NZ's seamers like Trent Boult, but SA's explosive hitters like Heinrich Klaasen could spark an upset if dew aids chasing; watch weather and toss for pivot points in this series opener.

question: T20 Series New Zealand vs South Africa: New Zealand vs South Africa - Toss Match Double New Zealand Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between New Zealand and South Africa scheduled for 2026-03-24 in T20 Series New Zealand vs South Africa.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to New Zealand will be considered correct if  New Zealand is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to South Africa will be considered correct if South Africa is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: T20 Series New Zealand vs South Africa: New Zealand vs South Africa - Most Sixes New Zealand Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between New Zealand and South Africa scheduled for 2026-03-24 in T20 Series New Zealand vs South Africa.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to New Zealand will be considered correct if New Zealand is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than South Africa.The outcome corresponding to South Africa will be considered correct if South Africa is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than New Zealand.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

eventMetadata.context_description: Both New Zealand and South Africa enter their T20I showdown with potent six-hitting firepower in the middle order, fueling the dead-even trader consensus at 50% apiece for most sixes alongside a draw possibility. Tight head-to-head history and mutual rest advantages keep sentiment balanced, reflecting the wisdom of crowds in this high-variance sixes race.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the New Zealand vs South Africa T20 top team batter market remains deadlocked at 49.5% across NZL, ZAF, and draw due to evenly matched batting firepower and uncertain pitch dynamics at the series opener. New Zealand's explosive top order—led by Rachin Ravindra's recent 69 off 37 in T20Is and Finn Allen's power-hitting—counters South Africa's middle-order threats like Heinrich Klaasen's IPL form (381 runs at 191 strike rate) and Aiden Markram's consistency. eventMetadata.context_description: Afghanistan's surging ODI form has emerged as the key driver of trader consensus favoring them in the Afghanistan vs Sri Lanka ODI series markets, with implied probabilities on Polymarket hovering around 55-60% for an Afghan series win or strong performance. The hosts recently swept Bangladesh 2-0 in July 2024, showcasing Rashid Khan's spin dominance (15 wickets in recent outings) and Ibrahim Zadran's batting consistency, while Sri Lanka's campaign faltered with a 0-3 whitewash by India amid injuries to Wanindu Hasaranga and Dushmantha Chameera. Historically, Sri Lanka leads head-to-head 12-4, but Afghanistan's home edge in UAE conditions—spin-friendly pitches—and superior current ICC ranking (7th vs 8th) tilt sentiment their way. Watch for lineup confirmations and weather, as rain-shortened games amplify upset potential.

question: Legends Cricket League: Mumbai Spartans vs Konark Suryas Odisha
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Mumbai Spartans and Konark Suryas Odisha scheduled for March 22 2026 in Legends Cricket League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

description:
